name: Krita AI Diffusion
tagline: AI as a brush, not a slot machine
description: A plugin that embeds generative AI into a real digital-painting workflow: selection-based inpainting, live painting where the AI reinterprets your canvas as you draw, ControlNet guidance from scribbles and poses, regional prompts via layers, and 4K upscaling — all on a local ComfyUI backend.
why: The best answer to what AI-assisted art should feel like: open diffusion models inside a professional FOSS painting app, where the artist keeps layer-level control and nothing leaves the machine.

setup.requirements: Krita 5.2+; the plugin auto-installs a managed ComfyUI backend; 6GB VRAM minimum locally, cloud option for weak hardware

license.notes: Some models it can drive (e.g. FLUX.2 dev) carry non-commercial weight licenses — the plugin is open regardless.

limitations: Local quality depends on what fits in your VRAM, and CPU mode is impractical.
take: A preview of how creative software absorbs these models: not as a button that makes art, but as another tool that listens.
